title: Researcher-authored content rendered as raw HTML via marked + {@html} without sanitization (stored XSS)
evidence: apps/web/src/lib/services/markdownProcessor.ts:7-12,71,117 (marked.use with NO sanitizer; marked passes raw HTML through by default); consumed via {@html} at apps/web/src/lib/modules/questions/shared/BaseQuestion.svelte:296,305 (question title/description in fillout), apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/text-instruction/TextInstruction.svelte:159, apps/web/src/lib/wysiwyg/QuestionVisualRenderer.svelte:457,471,492,512,517 (via mediaHandling.processContentWithMediaSync -> processMarkdownContentSync, mediaHandling.ts:76-96), and apps/web/src/lib/fillout/components/ConsentScreen.svelte:110 ({@html content} with no processing at all). Contrast: apps/web/src/lib/modules/display/text/TextDisplay.svelte:6,66 correctly uses DOMPurify.sanitize, and isomorphic-dompurify is already a dependency (apps/web/package.json:84).
detail: processMarkdownContent/Sync parse questionnaire text (titles, descriptions, instructions, consent) to HTML with marked and no sanitize step, then Svelte injects it with {@html} into the public fillout runtime. Any HTML in that content (e.g. ``) executes in every participant's browser, and in the collaborative design
rec: Route ALL markdown/HTML that reaches {@html} through DOMPurify.sanitize (as TextDisplay already does), ideally centralized inside processMarkdownContent/Sync so every caller (BaseQuestion, TextInstruction, QuestionVisualRenderer, ConsentScreen) is covered by construction. Sanitize AFTER marked.parse and after variable interpolation.

title: Script sandbox escapable via bracket-notation constructor chain; ScriptExecutor runs on the main thread with DOM access and no timeout
evidence: apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/ScriptExecutor.ts:11-12 (BANNED_PROPERTY_ACCESS is a dot-only regex), :202-216 (Proxy get-trap only guards sandbox free-var lookups), :224-225 (new Function('sandbox','with(sandbox){...}') executed on the MAIN thread), :333-340 hooks invoked synchronously with no timeout. Same dot-only guard in packages/scripting-engine/src/ScriptWorker.ts:45,124,157-170. The security test itself documents the gap: apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/ScriptExecutor.security.test.ts:29-30,79-81 ('Property-chain access ... bypasses the Proxy — the ScriptWorker provides full isolation') — but ScriptExecutor does NOT use the Worker. PoC (run in this session) confirmed `[]['constructor']['constructor']('return this')()` passes BANNED_PROPERTY_ACCESS and returns the real global object.
detail: Researcher `question.settings.script` hooks execute via new Function+with(sandbox) directly on the participant's main thread. The regex only matches `.constructor`, so `['constructor']` (or `['con'+'structor']`) is not rejected, and the Proxy get-trap only intercepts scope-chain lookups — property access on a real literal like `[]` is ung
rec: Do not rely on regex+Proxy for isolation on the main thread. Move all researcher script execution into the Web Worker path (which at least has a hard terminate() timeout), and harden the worker sandbox against bracket-notation constructor access (deny computed member access to constructor/prototype, or evaluate via the AST evaluator only)

title: JWT access and refresh tokens stored in localStorage (XSS-exfiltratable, durable takeover)
evidence: apps/web/src/lib/services/auth.ts:11 (STORAGE_KEY='qdesigner-auth'), :322-334 storeSession writes accessToken AND refreshToken to localStorage as JSON, :336-345 read back on init. Token attached as Authorization: Bearer at apps/web/src/lib/api/runtime.ts:6-17 (no cookie auth).
detail: Both tokens are readable by any JS on the origin. Combined with the XSS (finding 1) and sandbox-escape (finding 2) vectors, an attacker exfiltrates the long-lived refresh token and gains persistent account takeover, not just a session-scoped one. The Bearer-only design is good for CSRF but pushes all trust onto XSS resistance, which the r
rec: Store the refresh token in an httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=strict cookie handled by the server; keep only a short-lived access token in memory (not localStorage). At minimum, get the refresh token out of JS-reachable storage.

title: Worker script sandbox also escapable; 'use strict' guard is inert
evidence: packages/scripting-engine/src/ScriptWorker.ts:45 (dot-only BANNED_PATTERN), :157-170 (get-trap only on sandbox proxy), :172-173 (wrappedCode = '"use strict";' + script placed INSIDE `with(sandbox){...}`, so it is not a directive prologue and does not enable strict mode). Same pattern in packages/scripting-engine/src/ScriptEngine.ts:217.
detail: The same bracket-notation constructor bypass applies inside the Worker. Escape yields the Worker global scope (self) — no DOM, but fetch/XMLHttpRequest/importScripts are available, so the `context` passed in (variables, answers) can be exfiltrated to an attacker host from the participant's origin. The `"use strict"` intended to block impl
rec: Harden the worker sandbox against computed constructor/prototype access (not just dotted forms), and put the `"use strict"` at the true start of the Function body (or rely on the AST evaluator). Consider a strict CSP on the worker to block network egress.

title: Sensitive participant data unencrypted in IndexedDB; content and presigned URLs logged to console
evidence: apps/web/src/lib/services/db/indexeddb.ts:154-198 (filloutSessions/filloutResponses/filloutVariables stored in plaintext, no encryption); auth token also plaintext in localStorage (auth.ts:322). apps/web/src/lib/services/markdownProcessor.ts:171-208 console.log of full content, media array, and resolved media URLs (which for designer preview are presigned, signature-bearing).
detail: Offline-first fillout persists psychological-research responses unencrypted in the browser until synced/cleared — a concern on shared/kiosk devices common in research settings. Verbose console logging of content and presigned media URLs leaks data into the browser console.
rec: Purge fillout IndexedDB tables on completion/sync and on a shared-device 'end session' action; consider at-rest encryption for response data. Remove the debug console.log statements in markdownProcessor (they also log presigned URLs).

title: Rate limiting keyed on client-controlled X-Forwarded-For bypasses brute-force protection on auth endpoints
evidence: rate_limit.rs:86-97 — for requests with no AuthenticatedUser in extensions the key is request.headers().get("x-forwarded-for") used verbatim (fallback "unknown"). mod.rs:28-46 — auth_routes (login/register/verify-email/verify/send/resend/password-reset/confirm) attach ONLY the rate_limit layer; no auth extractor runs before it, so AuthenticatedUser is never present and the key is always the raw XFF header.
detail: The XFF header is fully attacker-controlled. Rotating its value yields a fresh max_requests-per-window bucket per request (Redis key ratelimit:{key}, in-memory HashMap keyed by the same string). Even behind an appending proxy ("attacker_val, real_ip") the attacker-controlled prefix keeps each key unique. This nullifies the only server-sid
rec: Derive the client identity from the socket peer address (ConnectInfo) or a trusted-proxy-count index into XFF; never key on the raw header. Combine with a per-account/per-email counter for auth endpoints.

title: No maximum password length (hardening gap) — stated Argon2 DoS mechanism is overstated
evidence: models.rs:50,76,118 — LoginRequest/RegisterRequest/PasswordResetConfirm validate only length(min=8), no max. password.rs:9-16 — hash_password feeds password.as_bytes() to Argon2::default().
detail: The code facts are correct: there is no max password length. But the claimed impact is wrong. Argon2's cost is fixed by its memory/time/parallelism parameters and is independent of input length — the password is folded into a single Blake2b pre-hash (H0) before the memory-hard phase, so a multi-MB password adds only a cheap linear Blake2b
rec: Add length(max = 128) (or similar) to the three password fields, primarily as input hygiene / body-size bounding. Do not rely on it to prevent Argon2 DoS — that framing is inaccurate.

title: WebSocket JWT transmitted in the URL query string
evidence: websocket/handler.rs:18-33 — WsQuery { token: String }; token read from /api/ws?token=<jwt> and passed to verify_access_token. main.rs:194 — TraceLayer::new_for_http().
detail: The access token is a query parameter on the upgrade GET (CWE-598). Real exposure is narrower than the finding states: a WebSocket handshake does not emit a Referer, and the URL is not navigated to so browser history is largely irrelevant; TraceLayer::new_for_http() records the URI at DEBUG (its default), so it is NOT emitted under the de
rec: Pass the token via the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol subprotocol header or an Authorization header on the upgrade, or issue a single-use short-TTL ticket exchanged out of band; keep bearer tokens out of URLs.

title: Email-verification code is 6 digits with no per-account attempt limit or lockout, and send has no rate cap or user-existence check
evidence: auth.rs:492-535 — verify_code does a single SELECT EXISTS on email+code with expires_at>NOW() and used_at IS NULL; no attempt counter, no lockout, no invalidation after N failures. auth.rs:394 — code = format!("{:06}", gen_range(100_000..1_000_000)). auth.rs:526 — on match, UPDATE users SET email_verified=true WHERE email=$1. auth.rs:388-409 — send_verification_code upserts a code for ANY email with no user-existence check and no send-rate cap.
detail: The 900k-value space over a 10-minute window is brute-forceable, and combined with finding #1's rate-limit bypass there is effectively no throttle. A successful guess sets email_verified=true for an arbitrary registered email (no auth required to call verify_code). send_verification_code also emails any address unconditionally (email-bomb
rec: Add a per-email attempt counter with lockout after ~5 failures, invalidate the code on lockout, lengthen the code or shorten TTL, and rate-limit send/resend per email independent of the IP-based limiter.

title: Anonymous session media upload has no size/content-type/magic-byte validation and full-buffers the file in memory
evidence: media.rs:526-591 — upload_session_media takes only State + Path<session_id> + Multipart (no AuthenticatedUser); it checks only that the session exists and status=='active' (:532-541), reads field.bytes().to_vec() into a Vec<u8> (:558-562), and builds the S3 key with the raw client filename (:571). No DefaultBodyLimit/RequestBodyLimit override exists (grep clean). upload_media (:135) shares the no-validation pattern.
detail: Any caller who knows an active session UUID (the URL-as-credential model) can upload arbitrary bytes with an unvalidated content_type and no MIME allowlist or magic-byte check, stored under a key containing the raw filename. Today the only bound is axum's implicit 2 MB DefaultBodyLimit; if raised/disabled for legitimate large stimuli the
rec: Require and enforce a content-length/size cap, a MIME allowlist with magic-byte sniffing, and sanitize/drop the client filename from the key; consider streaming to storage rather than buffering, and gate anonymous uploads behind the session's active questionnaire needing them.

title: RLS anonymous session-INSERT branches do not constrain sessions.user_id (defense-in-depth gap)
evidence: 00021_fillout_dual_policies.sql — sessions_insert_dual WITH CHECK admits via is_super_admin() OR user-GUC match OR session-GUC match (sessions.id = current_app_session_id()) OR the bootstrap branch (current_app_user_id() IS NULL AND current_app_session_id() IS NULL). None of the anonymous-admitting branches reference the user_id column. sessions.rs:409 — session_user_id = user.as_ref().map(|u| u.user_id) (NULL for anonymous), so the handler writes it correctly today.
detail: Confirmed and slightly broader than stated: not only the bootstrap branch but also the session-GUC branch admits an anonymous INSERT without checking the written user_id, so RLS — the layer meant to backstop a compromised/buggy handler — would not stop an anonymous INSERT that set user_id to a victim's id. No forgery is possible today bec
rec: Add WITH CHECK constraints so anonymous branches require sessions.user_id IS NULL and the user branch requires user_id = current_app_user_id().

title: Dev bootstrap endpoint enable-in-prod via env var, and verify_domain auto-verifies with no ownership proof
evidence: mod.rs:270-292 + dev.rs:44-65 — /api/dev/bootstrap-personas is mounted only when cfg!(debug_assertions) || DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED in {1,true,yes,on}, and the handler re-checks dev_helpers_enabled(). organizations.rs:1495-1527 — verify_domain requires OrgRole::Admin (has_org_role at :1505) then unconditionally UPDATE organization_domains SET verified_at=NOW() ("In development, auto-verify") with no DNS/ownership challenge.
detail: bootstrap-personas is double-gated and provisions fixed-UUID personas only from env-provided passwords, so it is not a default-cred backdoor — but a release build with DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED=true exposes deterministic persona provisioning. verify_domain lets any org admin mark ANY domain (including a public email provider) verified with zero
rec: Compile the dev router out of release builds (cfg(debug_assertions) only, drop the env escape hatch), and replace verify_domain's auto-verify with a real DNS TXT/HTTP ownership challenge before it backs any auto-join.

title: Login user-enumeration timing side channel
evidence: auth.rs:115-135 — on missing user the handler returns Unauthorized at :126 (fetch_optional...ok_or_else) and on missing password_hash at :131, both BEFORE verify_password runs at :133. Existing users always pay the Argon2 verify cost.
detail: The response body is identical ("Invalid email or password") but response time differs measurably: non-existent/no-hash accounts skip Argon2 verification, existent accounts do not. This enables email enumeration. Low impact alone; more useful combined with finding #1's rate-limit bypass.
rec: Perform a dummy Argon2 verify against a fixed decoy hash when the user/hash is absent so all paths pay comparable cost.

title: Quota management is hollow for multi-cell/demographic quotas — server returns total completions for every quota
evidence: sessions.rs:2706-2715 — explicit comment 'For now, return total_completed as the current count' then `current: total_completed, is_full: total_completed >= target` pushed for EVERY enabled quota inside the loop, ignoring each quota's `condition`. Client QuotaService.checkQuotas (QuotaService.ts:53-97) evaluates each quota's condition only against `currentValues` (the urlParams Map built at +page.svelte:419-424) and flags a quota full when `conditionMatches && status.isFull`. The status.current/isFull is the server's total, not the per-cell count.
detail: Fully confirmed on both server and client. Per-cell counting does not exist: every quota cell receives current=total_completed and is_full=(total>=cellTarget). A male respondent whose urlParam matches gender==male gets blocked once TOTAL completions reach the male cell's target, regardless of how many completions were actually male. Only
rec: Compute per-quota counts server-side by evaluating each quota's condition against completed sessions' stored demographic/response values (or maintain per-cell counters at completion time), and return real current/is_full per quota. Re-check quotas after in-survey demographic questions, not just from urlParams, and enforce on the offline/s

title: Question-level skip/branch/loop targets are a designer illusion — runtime resolves targets against pages only
evidence: FlowControlManager.svelte:42-56 pushes both page targets and question targets (label `Q: ...`, type 'question') into availableTargets. QuestionnaireRuntime.handleFlowControl (lines 1128-1168) resolves skip/branch (1157) and loop (1144) targets exclusively via `this.config.questionnaire.pages.findIndex(page => page.id === matchingRule.target)`; on -1 it falls through to `navigateToPage(currentPageIndex + 1)` (1168).
detail: Confirmed. Page ids and question ids are distinct namespaces, so a question-id target never matches a page, findIndex returns -1, and the branch/skip/loop silently no-ops into linear next-page navigation. Authors can wire screening/branching logic to a specific question in the designer and it will be discarded at fillout with no error. Me
rec: Either resolve question-id targets to their containing page (and optionally scroll/anchor to the question) in handleFlowControl, or restrict FlowControlManager's target list to pages only so the designer cannot offer targets the runtime can't honor.

title: Domain-based auto-join cannot be completed through the product — verification is a stub
evidence: admin/domains/+page.svelte:129-140 — handleVerifyDomain performs no DNS TXT / .well-known check; it only sets a `notice` string stating 'Automated domain verification is not available yet ... auto-join stays disabled for unverified domains.'
detail: Confirmed and honestly stubbed (the comment notes a prior version auto-verified unconditionally and was correctly removed). The Add Domain flow (success message at line 120) and updateDomainConfig with autoJoinEnabled exist, but domains remain Pending forever because no code path can transition them to Verified. The advertised org auto-jo
rec: Implement server-side DNS TXT or .well-known token verification and a verify endpoint, or clearly mark the feature as roadmap/hidden until then.

title: Onboarding invitation discovery disabled — invited users who skip the token link create a new org
evidence: onboarding/organization/+page.svelte:40-42 — '// For now, skip invitation check due to RLS issues / TODO: Fix RLS policies for organization_invitations table'; onMount unconditionally sets `showCreateForm = true` after the orgs.length check.
detail: Confirmed. Token-based accept (handleAcceptInvitation -> api.organizations.invitations.accept, lines 45-58) works, but the passive discovery path is bypassed: a user with a pending invitation who signs up without clicking the emailed token lands directly on the create-organization form. The practical consequence (tenancy fragmentation — i
rec: Resolve the RLS policy on organization_invitations so onboarding can list pending invitations by the user's email and offer join-vs-create.

title: Cross-project/org-wide analytics is wired at the API layer but has no UI entry point; local compute module is dead code
evidence: Server route exists: api/mod.rs:106 `/{id}/analytics -> sessions::cross_project_analytics`. Client method exists: api.ts:752-767 crossProjectAnalytics. Grep for `crossProjectAnalytics` across apps/web/src returns only its own definition (api.ts:754) — no route/component caller. Grep for `from '.../analytics/CrossProjectAnalytics'` / `CrossProjectAnalytics.getInstance` returns zero external importers; the class in lib/analytics/CrossProjectAnalytics.ts is unreferenced.
detail: Confirmed. A full server endpoint plus typed client method for org-wide cross-project analytics exist with no UI that invokes them, and the standalone CrossProjectAnalytics engine class has no importers (dead code). Low severity is correct — unsurfaced capability plus dead code, not a user-facing defect.
rec: Either surface the capability from the org analytics page (replace its per-questionnaire re-aggregation with the endpoint) or remove the unused client method and CrossProjectAnalytics.ts.

title: No self-service account deletion (right-to-erasure friction)
evidence: settings/+page.svelte:150-159 — Account card states 'Self-service account deletion is not yet available. To permanently delete your account ... contact your organization administrator or email support.' No delete action/button is rendered.
detail: Confirmed and honestly labeled. For a platform storing participant and researcher PII this is a GDPR right-to-erasure completeness gap, though it's a disclosed absence rather than a broken control. Low severity is appropriate.
rec: Add a self-service delete-account flow (with confirmation + backend cascade/anonymization) or document the manual erasure SLA.

title: Participant-facing fillout screens broken by incomplete Tailwind v4 HSL-triplet migration
evidence: variables.css declares tokens as bare HSL triplets (--muted: 210 40% 96.1%, --background/--card/--primary etc.). app.css:30-80 @theme inline confirms these only become colors via hsl(var(--token)). Scoped component CSS feeds the raw triplet directly: WelcomeScreen.svelte:136 background:var(--background), :193 .instructions{background:var(--muted)}; ConsentScreen.svelte:189/253/278 background:var(--muted|--background), :271/318 color:var(--destructive), :295 border 1px solid var(--border); LanguagePicker.svelte:56/79; +page.svelte:786 .loading-progress{background:var(--muted)}, :794 .progress-bar{background:var(--primary)}, :856 .form-card{background:var(--card,#ffffff)}. All resolve to invalid values like 'background: 210 40% 96.1%' and are dropped. CompletionScreen.svelte:161-277 correctly uses semantic utilities (bg-background, bg-muted, text-muted-foreground, border-border), proving an incomplete migration.
detail: The .form-card fallback var(--card,#ffffff) never engages because --card IS defined (as a triplet), so the container hosting every form-style question renders with no background over the canvas; the load progress bar, muted surface boxes (.instructions/.consent-checkboxes/.signature-section), input borders and the language-picker active p
rec: Migrate these scoped styles to either the semantic utilities (bg-muted, text-muted-foreground, border-border, bg-primary) as CompletionScreen already does, or wrap each token in hsl(var(--token)). Grep the fillout tree for 'var(--' color usages.

title: Native alert()/confirm() dialogs bypass the styled Dialog/Toast system
evidence: 13 .svelte files use alert()/confirm(). Verified: ConsentScreen.svelte:95 confirm() in participant decline flow; QuestionCard.svelte:22, BlockManager.svelte:147, VariableManager.svelte:96, ScriptEditorOverlay.svelte:84, MediaManager.svelte:254/373, Designer.svelte:74 alert('Invalid questionnaire file'); admin/domains:167, admin/invitations:127, admin/users:68/101, invite/[token]:87. A styled Dialog (lib/components/ui/overlays/Dialog.svelte) and Toast (lib/components/ui/Toast.svelte + lib/stores/toast.ts) exist.
detail: Native dialogs are unstyleable, ignore dark mode and block the main thread — most jarring in the participant consent-decline path and for surfacing errors. This is a consistency/polish defect, not a functional break.
rec: Replace destructive confirmations with the Dialog component and error notices with the Toast store.

title: Designer drag-drop is touch-hostile (HTML5 DnD), but responsive fallbacks exist
evidence: WYSIWYGCanvas.svelte:203-204 uses ondrop/ondragover (HTML5 DnD, does not fire on touch). Designer +page:273-303 is a fixed h-screen three-column layout. BUT the claim overstates impact: BOTH sidebars become mobile drawers — LeftSidebar.svelte:37 AND RightSidebar.svelte:40 use matchMedia('(max-width:767px)') with dedicated drawer markup (LeftSidebar:207, RightSidebar:174). A click/tap add path also exists: WYSIWYGCanvas empty-state onclick=openAddPanel (:256, fn at :182), plus DesignerCommandPalette.addQuestion. So touch users are NOT hitting a 'silently dead canvas'.
detail: The genuine defect is narrower than stated: dragging a question type from the palette and drag-reordering are unusable on touch, and there's no tap-to-reorder affordance. But adding questions works via the add panel / command palette, and the columns collapse to drawers rather than colliding, so the finding's 'effectively desktop-only / n
rec: Add a pointer:coarse move-up/down affordance for reordering; the add flow already degrades acceptably via openAddPanel.

title: ~250 console.* statements ship to production incl. participant runtime and PII
evidence: grep counted 250 console.* across lib+routes (analyst said ~258; close). 25 within lib/fillout + routes/(fillout). Verified leftovers: projects/+page.svelte:169 console.log('Navigating to:',...); +layout.svelte:23-31 '[Layout] Starting module registration...'; login/+page.svelte:186 console.log('Login successful, user:', signedInUser.email) — logs the user's email (PII).
detail: Debug logging persists on the anonymous participant surface and around auth. The login PII log is the sharpest concrete instance.
rec: Strip debug logs (or gate behind a dev flag / logger) especially in fillout and auth paths; never log email.

title: Project create modal uses raw inputs, full-page reloads, and lacks in-flight submit feedback
evidence: projects/+page.svelte:234-268 hand-rolls <input>/<textarea> with duplicated utility-class strings instead of the shared Input component; :64-65 and :171 navigate via window.location.href (full reload) rather than goto; Create button disabled={!newProjectName.trim() || !newProjectCode.trim()} — never disables while the request is in flight.
detail: Diverges from the design system (login uses Input + Button loading prop). The hard reload flashes the page and the always-enabled-during-submit button invites double-submits with no spinner. Note the modal IS a Dialog with a footer snippet, so the wrapper is fine; only the fields/nav/loading-state are the issue.
rec: Use Input/FormGroup, goto() for SPA nav, and Button's loading prop tied to the in-flight state.

title: Skeleton primitive nearly unused across async surfaces
evidence: lib/components/ui/Skeleton.svelte exists but is referenced in only 2 components besides its definition: SaveLoadToolbar.svelte and VersionManager.svelte. Dashboard/projects async surfaces render straight from load data or pop in post-mount.
detail: A missed-consistency opportunity rather than a defect — perceived load quality is uneven (layout shift / pop-in) on data-heavy views.
rec: Apply Skeleton to dashboard stat cards, sparklines, and list loads.

title: God components concentrate interaction logic
evidence: Line counts verified exactly: ReactionLabWorkspace.svelte 1959, PropertiesPanel.svelte 1108, StatisticsBuilder.svelte 891, MediaManager.svelte 698.
detail: Not a user-visible bug; raises the odds of divergent one-off UI (validation/feedback/keyboard handling) drifting from the rest of the designer over time.
rec: Extract sub-panels/hooks incrementally when touched.

title: $derived(() => …) used as a callable value — defeats memoization (14 sites)
evidence: CompletionScreen.svelte:112 `const effectiveRedirectUrl = $derived(() => redirectUrl() || panelRedirectUrl())` invoked as `effectiveRedirectUrl()` at :116; same pattern at :46/:54/:63/:94. Confirmed at ConsentScreen.svelte:58 (called :70), TextDisplay.svelte:128 (called :150 `style={contentStyle()}`), IRTPanel.svelte:61/:76 (tifData() called in tifPath at :77), QuestionRenderer.svelte:131, WelcomeScreen.svelte:39. `grep '$derived((…) =>'` = 14 hits; `$derived.by` = 45 hits, confirming the codebase knows the correct form.
detail: Each `const x = $derived(() => expr)` wraps a function literal that reads no reactive state at creation, so the derived has no dependencies, memoizes a stable function reference once, and re-runs the full body on every `x()` call. Reactivity still flows because the reads happen inside the consumer's effect/render scope, so there is no cor
rec: Replace `$derived(() => …)` with `$derived.by(() => …)` and drop the call parens at use sites, or inline as a plain expression where trivial.

title: Half-migrated QuestionCard.svelte is a latent reactivity trap
evidence: QuestionCard.svelte:6-10 props via `export let question/index/isSelected/blockId/pageId`; :12-13 `let isDragOver = false; let dropPosition: 'before'|'after'|null = null` as bare reactive locals; :156-169 modern Svelte 5 `ondragover`/`ondragleave`/`ondrop`/`ondragstart`/`onclick`/`onkeydown` attributes; drop indicators keyed on `isDragOver`/`dropPosition` at :162 and :301. Zero runes present in the file. Live via PageCanvas.svelte per the finding.
detail: The component runs in Svelte 4 legacy mode (no runes, so `let` locals are still reactive) while already using Svelte 5 event attributes. It compiles and works today. The trap: adding any rune later flips the file to runes mode, at which point `isDragOver`/`dropPosition` become plain non-reactive variables and the drag highlight/drop indic
rec: Finish the migration: `$props()` for the five props, `$state()` for isDragOver/dropPosition.

title: Cluster of fully-legacy Svelte 4 routes/components still shipping (incl. core login)
evidence: login/+page.svelte:23-27 bare `let` state, :36-37 `$:` + `$page` (0 runes in file); admin/users/+page.svelte:23-24 `$:`, :231 `on:change`, :254 `on:click`; admin/invitations/+page.svelte:210 `<form on:submit|preventDefault=…>`; admin/domains/+page.svelte:226 same modifier syntax; ThemeProvider.svelte:15-16 `export let` + :99 `<slot />`; style-guide/+page.svelte:6 `$app/stores` + `$:` at :6.
detail: All verified rune-free, so they compile in legacy mode. They include the core auth login and admin member/role/domain management. Each carries the same silent-break risk as QuestionCard if a rune is later added, and `on:submit|preventDefault` has no runes-mode equivalent, so a naive migration must rewrite the handler to call `e.preventDef
rec: Migrate these to runes ($props/$state/$derived) and native event handlers; explicitly handle the preventDefault modifier during conversion.

title: Config-default $effects mutate bound props instead of normalizing on load
evidence: MediaResponseDesigner.svelte:53-61 `$effect` writes seven `question.config.*` defaults; BarChart.svelte:75-82 `$effect` sets `analytics.dataSource = {…}`; DrawingDesigner.svelte:59-… `$effect` seeds `question.config = {…}`. Each is guarded (`if (!question.config.x)` / `if (!analytics.dataSource)`) so it self-terminates.
detail: These seed parent-owned prop objects from inside the reactive graph. Guards prevent looping, but writing to props in `$effect` entangles initialization with reactivity, re-runs on every dependency change, and mutates state owned by the parent as a side effect — 'derive-with-fallback' logic forced through an effect. Correct today; low.
rec: Normalize defaults once at construction (in the parent factory / on load) or read through a `$derived` fallback rather than mutating props in an effect.

title: Effect writes state that a $derived should own
evidence: AppLoader.svelte:64-69 `$effect` assigns `currentStage` (a `$state`) purely from `stages.find(s => s.status==='loading')`. PropertiesPanel.svelte:366-370 `$effect` resets `bulkOptionDraft = formatChoiceOptions(questionItem)` when `questionItem && isChoiceQuestion`.
detail: AppLoader is a textbook derived-as-effect adding a redundant $state cell and reactivity hop — CONFIRMED. One nuance the analyst glossed: the effect only assigns when an activeStage exists (guard at :66), so it retains the last message when nothing is loading; a straight `$derived` swap would need an explicit fallback, so it is not a 1:1 r
rec: Make currentStage a `$derived` (with a fallback for no active stage). For PropertiesPanel, key the reset on selected-item identity, not deep questionItem changes, to avoid clobbering the draft.

title: Deprecated $app/stores ($page) used across ~10 modules
evidence: `import { page } from '$app/stores'` confirmed in (app)/+layout.svelte:2, (app)/+error.svelte:2, (fillout)/+error.svelte:2, AppShell.svelte:2, SaveLoadToolbar.svelte:4, VersionManager.svelte:5, style-guide/+page.svelte:6, plus login/+page.svelte (uses `$page.url`).
detail: All read navigation state via the deprecated auto-subscription `$app/stores` / `$page` idiom. SvelteKit 2.12+ prefers fine-grained `$app/state` (`page.url`, no `$`). Works today; keeps these files on the store-subscription mechanism the rest of the codebase moved away from. Low.
rec: Migrate to `import { page } from '$app/state'` and `page.url` (drop the `$`).

title: ThemeProvider defines a competing writable theme store with a never-unsubscribed subscription
evidence: ThemeProvider.svelte:1-8 module-context `writable` `themeMode`/`customTheme`; :72-76 instance-level `themeMode.subscribe(mode => { setThemeMode(mode); })` with no captured/called unsubscribe; `grep ThemeProvider` shows the sole importer is StyleGuide.svelte (transitively only the dev /style-guide route).
detail: Confirmed dead-weight: a legacy `writable` theme store parallel to the live `$lib/stores/theme.ts`, plus an uncleaned instance subscription that would leak on remount. Two clarifications to avoid overstating: (1) the subscribe callback calls setThemeMode → themeMode.set, but Svelte's safe_not_equal short-circuits equal primitives, so it i
rec: Delete ThemeProvider (and StyleGuide's use of it) or fold onto the live theme store; if kept, capture and call the unsubscribe in onDestroy.

title: Ambient god-store singleton is the dominant coupling mechanism; zero Context API isolation
evidence: designer.svelte.ts:1132 ends with `export const designerStore = new DesignerStore()`. grep: 31 .svelte files import designerStore; `grep -rhoE 'designerStore\.\w+' | sort -u` = exactly 91 distinct members; 23 `$state` fields (lines 110-148, the 24th `$state` hit is a comment on line 36); ~52-60 methods. `setContext(` = 0 matches repo-wide; every `getContext` hit is `canvas.getContext('webgl2'/'2d')`, none is Svelte context. RightSidebar.svelte:144 and :197 render `<PropertiesPanel />` with no props; PropertiesPanel reads state off the singleton directly.
detail: Confirmed exactly. The counts are accurate (91 distinct members verbatim, 23 state fields, 0 setContext). This is a genuine architectural constraint: no injection seam, so designer components cannot be mounted in isolation or driven by a mock. Caveat: the analyst's linkage to 'the Phase 4 TODO (handler/round-trip tests missing)' is a slig
rec: Introduce a Context API seam (setContext/getContext) wrapping the designer store so components receive it via context, enabling isolated mounting and a mockable boundary; optionally narrow the surface by splitting the 91-member store into role-scoped sub-stores exposed through context.

title: Two parallel reaction designer stacks duplicate block/trial/phase editing; one escapes the module-registry contract
evidence: reaction-time/metadata.ts:27 registers ReactionTimeDesigner.svelte (1704 lines), which imports ./designer/BlockEditor (dir contains BlockEditor, TrialTemplateEditor, ResponseMappingEditor, PhaseTimelineEditor). reaction-experiment/metadata.ts:24 registers the 48-line ReactionExperimentDesigner.svelte, whose only action is openLab() → `designerStore.openReactionLab(question.id)` (line 19). The real editor ReactionLabWorkspace.svelte (1959 lines) is invoked NOT via registry but via route fork at +page.svelte:286 (`{:else if designerStore.reactionLabQuestion}`, mounted line 287). The registry escape hatch is real: ReactionExperimentDesigner renders in PropertiesPanel like every other module but merely sets a global flag (reactionLabQuestionId) that a route-level special case reads to take over the whole canvas — the s
rec: Extract the shared block/trial/phase sub-editors into a reusable set consumed by both stacks, and either fold ReactionLabWorkspace back under the ModuleComponents.designer contract or formalize the full-canvas takeover as a documented capability rather than an ad-hoc route fork.

Note it does import MediaManagerModal (so it reuses that modal), yet still carries additional inline asset-picker wiring — the a

rec: Adopt Input/Button/FormGroup across designer and route components; add a lint rule flagging raw `<input>`/`<button>` outside the ui package to arrest regression.

Low severi

(2) Even if it read the c

Condition is seeded/recomputable; the quality report is partially recomputable (flatliner from stored

title: Redundant unconditional server round-trips fire for offline/client-UUID sessions
evidence: FilloutRuntime.ts:82,88-94 startSession() calls SessionManagementService.startSession unconditionally (not navigator.onLine-guarded); SessionManagementService.ts:117-124 issues api.sessions.update({status:'in_progress',...}). ResponsePersistenceService.ts:129-144 updateSessionProgress → api.sessions.update on every response. All wrapped in try/catch with console.error. The defused ordering hazard comment is at FilloutRuntime.ts:218-225.
detail: Confirmed: both calls are unconditional and fire-and-forget. For an offline-created client-UUID session with no server row, these PUTs hit a non-existent session once connectivity returns. The exact status code (404 vs 401/403 for anonymous callers) is inferred, not proven from this code, but the calls are demonstrably wasted effort dupli
rec: Guard these updates behind an online + server-session-exists check, or remove them since FilloutSyncEngine already materializes the session idempotently at sync time.

title: Variable formulas using RANDOM()/NOW() are non-deterministic — offline recompute does not hold for them
evidence: VariableEngine.ts:24 NOW=()=>Date.now(); :25 TIME_SINCE=Date.now()-timestamp; :34 RANDOM=()=>Math.random(); :35 RANDINT=Math.floor(Math.random()*...). Unseeded. (Cited as 24-35; exact.)
detail: Confirmed. Block/question randomization is seeded/deterministic (BlockRandomizer + settings.randomizationSeed||id), but these formula builtins use unseeded Math.random / Date.now, so any variable formula depending on them cannot be reproduced from persisted inputs. This is inherent to the function semantics, not an offline-specific defect
rec: If reproducibility is required for such variables, persist the computed values (see #1/#2) rather than relying on recompute, and/or seed RANDOM/RANDINT from the session seed.

title: DB constraint violations are never mapped — concurrent duplicate inserts surface as 500 instead of 409
evidence: 0 occurrences of as_database_error/is_unique_violation/.code() across apps/server/src. auth.rs register: SELECT EXISTS(...users WHERE email=$1) at line 36, ApiError::Conflict at 42, then plain INSERT INTO users at 54 with no ON CONFLICT (grep confirms ON CONFLICT appears only at auth.rs:402 for verification codes). error.rs:59-64 routes sqlx::Error to generic 500.

Both are fully `any`, so field renames, wrong option shapes, or missing prompts between the engine and modules produce zero com

title: api client discards generated contract types via `raw: any` mappers and `as unknown as` casts
evidence: services/api.ts: ~15 `map*(raw: any)` functions (mapAggregateData:156, mapCompareData:167, mapCrossProjectAnalytics:193, mapOrganization:231, …) doing unchecked `raw.snake ?? raw.camel` reads; `as unknown as Promise<SessionData>` at :1064, :1090, :1111, :1121; `sessions.sync` at :1293 `body: Record<string, unknown>` then `body as GeneratedSyncPayload` at :1300. All verified.
detail: Every mapper laundering a generated payload through `raw: any` loses the compile-time contract: a server field rename yields a silent `undefined`→default with no error. The strongest genuine drift vector is `sessions.sync` (the ADR-0023 D2 offline write path): its body is accepted as `Record<string, unknown>` and blind-cast to `GeneratedS
rec: Type mapper inputs against the generated response types (`raw: OrganizationResponse` etc.) so field renames break the build; type `sessions.sync` body as `GeneratedSyncPayload` directly instead of `Record<string, unknown>` + cast.

title: Designer god components bypass the shipped discriminated-union type guards with `(question as any)`
evidence: PropertiesPanel.svelte:279-281 `(question as any).responseType/.response/.display`; :152 `updateQuestion(updates: Partial<Question> & { config?: any })` writing back `as any` (:182, :184); type guards DO exist (`isSingleChoiceQuestion` at questionnaire.ts:1080, `isMultipleChoiceQuestion` :1084 — analyst said 1056+, off by ~24 lines but guards confirmed present).
detail: questionnaire-core ships a full discriminated `Question` union with type guards, but the largest designer components cast to `any` to reach `display`/`responseType`/`response` then write back `as any`, turning the property/block editors that mutate the authored model into an unchecked surface. I verified PropertiesPanel and questionnaire.
rec: Narrow via the existing type guards before touching type-specific fields; type `updateQuestion` config against a real config type; if Block is genuinely missing randomization/loop/conditions, add them rather than casting.

title: `DynamicValue = any` baked into the domain base type partially opens the otherwise-strong union
evidence: questionnaire.ts:52 `type DynamicValue = any`; BaseQuestion uses it at :808 (attentionCheck.correctAnswer), :813 `settings?: Record<string, DynamicValue>`, :814 `response?: DynamicValue // Loosely typed to allow access before narrowing`, :815 validation, :817 stimulus, :818 responseOptions. Verified (analyst's cited :811 for settings is actually :813, :816-818 is :815-818 — minor line drift, content confirmed).
detail: The discriminated union is well-designed but its base carries deliberate `any` holes (explicitly commented 'Loosely typed to allow access before narrowing') so callers can touch response/stimulus/settings pre-narrowing. This is precisely what lets the god-component `(question as any)` accesses typecheck, and it spreads `any` through anyth
rec: Replace `DynamicValue` on structured fields with `unknown` (forces narrowing at use sites) and reserve `any` only where genuinely open; push type-specific fields down into the union members instead of the base.

title: SessionManagementService sends snake_case payloads through `as any`, masking a possible contract mismatch
evidence: fillout/services/SessionManagementService.ts:90 `const session = data as unknown as Session`; :98-102, :118-123, :131-136, :143-147, :157-164 all `api.sessions.update(id, { last_activity_at/started_at/state_snapshot, status: 'not_started'|'paused'|... } as any)`; :277 `getLocalSession(): any`. All verified.
detail: api.sessions.update expects `Partial<SessionData>`; this service passes snake_case fields and status literals ('not_started','paused','abandoned') through `as any`, so TS cannot confirm those fields/enum values exist in the contract. A `Session` type distinct from `SessionData` is introduced via `as unknown as Session`, so two session sha
rec: Reconcile the `Session`/`SessionData` shapes into one type, make `sessions.update` accept the actual server wire shape, and drop the `as any` so field/enum typos surface at compile time.

title: scripting-engine `any` is confined to editor tooling / worker declarations (low runtime risk)
evidence: ScriptEngine.ts:232,235,250,254 `Record<string, any>`/`any[]` verified to live inside the template string returned by `getTypeDefinitions()` (Monaco IntelliSense .d.ts blob); types.ts:41 `implementation: (...args: any[]) => unknown` verified as a documented variadic function-table signature. MonacoConfig.ts lines not re-opened but the pattern (untyped monaco-editor API) is credible.
detail: Correctly assessed as non-hazardous: the ScriptEngine `any`s are text inside a declare-const string used only for editor autocomplete, carrying no runtime type risk; the function-table `(...args: any[]) => unknown` is a reasonable heterogeneous-implementation signature with an eslint-disable explaining callers narrow via `FormulaFunction.
rec: No action required; optionally tighten the function-table implementation type per-category if a stricter dispatch is desired later.

title: Fillout advances between questions without moving focus or announcing the change
evidence: (fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte:663 `{#key activePresentation.item.id}` re-mounts ModularRenderer; submitOverlayAnswer at :229-234 only calls presentation.onSubmit. A full-file grep for `aria-live|role=|.focus(|tabindex|<h1|<h2` on the fillout +page.svelte returned ZERO matches — there is no focus management, live region, or heading anywhere on the runtime screen.
detail: The {#key} block tears down and recreates the entire ModularRenderer subtree on every question. The previously-focused Continue button is removed, so keyboard focus falls to <body> and the user must re-Tab from the top each question; no live region announces the new question. The page's own status-bar (562-594) is the only region and carr
rec: On question change, move focus programmatically to the new question region (tabindex=-1 + .focus()) or a heading, and add an aria-live=polite region announcing question N of M / the new prompt.

title: Keyboard focus is invisible on multiple-choice and matrix radio/checkbox controls
evidence: MultipleChoice.svelte:464-468 `.choice-input { position:absolute; opacity:0; pointer-events:none }`; the only `focus:` in the file (:387) is on the free-text 'other' input, not the choice. Matrix.svelte:413-417/446-450 hide `.radio-input`/`.checkbox-input`; grep of both components shows `:checked +` and text-input `focus:` rules only — no `:focus`/`:focus-within` rule tied to the hidden input's indicator.
detail: Native radio/checkbox inputs are used (arrow/space work), but the input is opacity:0 and the custom indicator styles only :checked/:hover. A keyboard user sees no focus ring on either of the two most common answer types — WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible, AA) failure on the highest-traffic controls. pointer-events:none affects only the mouse, so
rec: Add `.choice-input:focus-visible + .choice-indicator` (and Matrix equivalents) rules that draw a visible ring, mirroring the design-system Button's focus-visible treatment.

title: Participant-facing charts render as bare <canvas> with no text alternative
evidence: FeedbackChart.svelte:642-643 `<canvas bind:this={canvasEl}>` with no role/aria/table; BarChart.svelte:524 and :536 `<canvas class="chart-canvas">` likewise; BaseAnalytics wrapper has no aria/heading/title. The canvas elements genuinely carry zero accessibility affordances.
detail: The canvas-has-no-text-alternative claim is CONFIRMED (WCAG 1.1.1 applies to the chart, and colorRules encode meaning with no non-color channel). BUT the analyst OVERSTATED impact: StatisticalFeedback.svelte renders substantial adjacent text — an <h3> title (:193), subtitle (:195), a numeric summary block (`summaryEntries`, :257-266) and
rec: Give each canvas role=img + an aria-label summarizing the result, and/or a visually-hidden data table. For BarChart specifically add a text summary since it has no adjacent text at all.

title: WebGL reaction stimuli have no reduced-motion or photosensitivity handling
evidence: grep for `reduced-motion|prefers-reduced|photosensitiv|epilep` across lib/runtime/reaction and lib/renderer returns NONE. The only guard is app.css:217 `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` which sets animation/transition-duration/scroll-behavior only — cannot touch canvas/rAF. ISI presets confirmed: stroop.ts:111=250, dotProbe.ts:113=500, flanker.ts:132=250 (also iat.ts:140=250, nBack.ts:84=250). Fillout canvas (:651-657) has no role/aria.
detail: Confirmed exactly. The global CSS motion guard is purely declarative CSS and does nothing for the WebGL requestAnimationFrame stimulus loop that presents rapidly alternating high-contrast stimuli at short ISIs. No prefers-reduced-motion branch in the engine, no photosensitive-content advisory before the task, no canvas label. Reaction tas
rec: Add a pre-task photosensitivity advisory and, where paradigm allows, a prefers-reduced-motion accommodation (longer ISIs / reduced flashing); label the canvas.

title: Question prompts are non-heading <div>s; no heading structure on the fillout screen
evidence: BaseQuestion.svelte:295 `<div class="question-title" id="question-{question.id}-title">` inside role="region" (:272) with aria-labelledby (:273). Full-file grep of the fillout +page.svelte found no <h1>/<h2> at all.
detail: Confirmed. Each question is a labelled region (reasonable), but the prompt is a div not a heading and the runtime page has no heading hierarchy or page-level H1 naming the study. Screen-reader heading navigation has nothing to jump to, compounding the #1 focus gap.
rec: Render the question title as a heading (e.g. role=heading/aria-level or an <h2>) and add a page-level H1 identifying the study.

title: Form fields do not wire aria-describedby to hints/errors or set aria-invalid
evidence: FormGroup.svelte:46 emits `id="{id}-error"`, :50 emits `id="{id}-hint"`. Input.svelte (props :2-25, markup :64-86) exposes no aria-describedby/aria-invalid and only swaps ring color on error (:55-58, `ring-destructive`). Select.svelte grep for `aria-|describedby|invalid` returns NONE.
detail: Confirmed. FormGroup generates stable error/hint ids implying intent to associate, but neither primitive references them via aria-describedby, and neither sets aria-invalid when error is true. The error is signalled by ring color only — a color-only cue (WCAG 1.4.1) — and is not programmatically linked (WCAG 1.3.1/3.3.1). Screen-reader us
rec: Add aria-describedby (pointing at the FormGroup error/hint ids) and aria-invalid props to Input/Select, and pair the color cue with text/icon.

title: Scale control mixes an ARIA slider role with discrete buttons
evidence: Scale.svelte:153 container `role="slider"` (tabindex=0, aria-valuemin/max :154-155) wraps `displayType==='buttons'` <button>s (:201, aria-pressed) and `stars` <button>s (:229). aria-valuenow=`value || question.config.min` (:156) so unanswered reports min. Visual-analog range input is `opacity-0` (:266); the only focus style is `.scale-container:focus` (:282), which does not apply when the inner range holds focus.
detail: Confirmed. A role=slider containing multiple focusable buttons is invalid ARIA (button variants should be a radiogroup); aria-valuenow always reports a concrete number, misrepresenting the unanswered state; and the opacity-0 range in the visual-analog variant has no visible focus because the container :focus rule can't match the inner inp
rec: Use role=radiogroup + role=radio for the button/star variants; only keep role=slider for the true range variant, style :focus-visible on the actual range input, and omit/adjust aria-valuenow when unset.

title: CommandPalette lacks dialog semantics, listbox pattern, and focus trap
evidence: CommandPalette.svelte:248 palette container is a plain <div> (no role=dialog/aria-modal/aria-label); backdrop button has aria-label (:244) but the panel does not. Results (:290) are <button>s with no role=option/aria-selected; search input (:257-264) has no role=combobox/aria-activedescendant. close() (:182-186) only resets state — no focus restore. No focus trap. Active item tracked solely by `bg-accent` class (:294).
detail: Confirmed on every sub-claim. Arrow-key selection has no programmatic exposure, so a screen-reader user typing in the box is not told which result is active; Tab is not trapped so focus can leave the visually-covering overlay; focus is not returned to the trigger on close. Designer-facing, so medium is correct.
rec: Add role=dialog/aria-modal/aria-label to the panel, implement the combobox+listbox pattern (aria-activedescendant, role=option/aria-selected), trap Tab within the panel, and restore focus to the opener on close.

title: Heavy reliance on muted-foreground for small text is borderline on contrast
evidence: themes/variables.css:7 light `--muted-foreground: 215.4 16.3% 46.9%` (= approx #64748b / slate-500). Computed relative luminance ~0.171 → contrast ~4.76:1 on pure white, ~4.57:1 on the actual `--background: 210 40% 98%` (:3). Used for hints (FormGroup :50), descriptions, placeholders, and Scale/summary text at 0.7–0.875rem throughout.
detail: Confirmed as an advisory: the value PASSES WCAG AA (4.5:1) but only by a thin margin (~4.57–4.76:1), and it is used pervasively for the smallest text and placeholders. It is not a failure today; the risk is regression if any muted-surface background darkens behind it. Correctly framed as low/advisory by the analyst.
rec: Add an explicit contrast check to CI/design tokens and slightly darken --muted-foreground (or forbid it on non-background surfaces) to buy margin above 4.5:1.

title: Full mathjs (create(all)) eagerly bundled + instantiated on public fillout route
evidence: VariableEngine.ts:7-8 `const factories = all; const math = create(factories)` runs at module top level. Static import chain proven: (fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte:11 statically imports FilloutRuntime; FilloutRuntime.ts:4 statically imports VariableEngine and constructs it at :54; QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:19 imports VariableEngine, :123 `new VariableEngine()` in ctor. vite.config.ts:73 `if (id.includes('mathjs')) return 'mathjs'` isolates the chunk but the import is static so the chunk is a hard route dependency.
detail: create(all) explicitly pulls the entire mathjs factory map (no tree-shaking) — matrices, complex, units, symbolic derivative/simplify — even though import/createUnit/simplify/derivative are then stubbed to throw via BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS. The module-level side effect runs once when the route JS evaluates, so every participant on every questio
rec: Lazy-load the mathjs-backed VariableEngine behind a dynamic import() gated on the questionnaire actually declaring computed variables, or replace create(all) with a create({...}) of only the used factories to enable tree-shaking.

title: N+1 query loop in compare-analytics endpoint
evidence: sessions.rs:1036 `for &qid in &questionnaire_ids` with per-iteration awaits: summary GROUP BY query at :1042 (.fetch_optional at :1064), timing query at :1073 (.fetch_all at :1083), and conditional load_numeric_samples at :1095. Valid-id set fetched once with `q.id = ANY($2)` at :1002-1016.
detail: Each questionnaire costs 2 serial round-trips (summary + timing), or 3 when a variable key is supplied (+ samples load), all awaited sequentially inside one request transaction. Comparing 10 questionnaires = ~20-30 serial DB round-trips. The pattern is a genuine N+1 fan-out. One caveat on severity: this is an authenticated admin/analytics
rec: Collapse to set-based queries using `WHERE questionnaire_id = ANY($ids) GROUP BY questionnaire_id` for summary and timing, and batch the samples load, then bucket results in Rust — turning 2-3N round-trips into ~3 total.

title: Offline-sync upsert issues one round-trip per response/event/variable
evidence: sessions.rs:2932-2955 per-response INSERT loop (`.execute(&mut **tx)` at :2949), :2958-2978 per-event INSERT loop (:2972), :2981-3002 per-variable persist loop (persist_session_variable at :2991). Each iteration is a single-row awaited execute.
detail: This is the FilloutSyncEngine.syncNow write path. A reaction-time experiment flushed after being offline can carry dozens-to-hundreds of trial responses plus interaction events; each row is a separate serialized INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING round-trip. Atomicity is fine (single tx), but wall-clock latency scales linearly
rec: Batch via multi-row INSERT (UNNEST arrays or QueryBuilder push_values) preserving the ON CONFLICT dedup, or sqlx COPY, to collapse N round-trips into a handful.

title: WebGLRenderer allocates per-frame and per-draw on the reaction-timing hot path
evidence: WebGLRenderer.ts:432 `Array.from(this.renderablesByLayer.keys()).sort(...)` every renderFrame; :367 and :430 two createRenderContext object allocations per frame (:409-418 returns fresh object); :368-374 FrameSample object per frame; drawGeometry :786 toClipSpace returns new number[]; :789 and :793 two `new Float32Array(...)` per draw with gl.DYNAMIC_DRAW re-upload.
detail: All cited allocations exist as described. renderFrame rebuilds+sorts the layer-key array every rAF tick regardless of set changes, and builds two RenderContext objects (one for frameCallbacks at :367, one inside renderFrame at :430) plus a FrameSample each frame; drawGeometry allocates a clip-space array and two typed arrays per draw and
rec: Hoist a reusable RenderContext/FrameSample and a cached sorted-layer array (invalidated on renderable add/remove), and reuse preallocated Float32Arrays / persistent VBOs (or bufferSubData) in drawGeometry.

title: ~258 console.* calls ship because the production build strips none
evidence: grep of src/lib + src/routes (excluding *.test./*.spec.) = 258 console.* calls; 39 within lib/fillout + lib/runtime. vite.config.ts:67 `minify: mode === 'production' ? 'esbuild' : false` with no `esbuild: { drop: ['console'] }` and no `define`/`pure` config anywhere in the file.
detail: Counts reproduced exactly (258 total, 39 in fillout/runtime hot path). esbuild minify does not drop console by default, so all statements ship to production — mild bundle bloat, real string-formatting cost when hit in per-question/timing loops, and a minor info-leak surface (internal state in participant consoles). Low is correct.
rec: Add `esbuild: { drop: ['console', 'debugger'] }` (or a guarded logger) for production builds; keep intentional user-facing warnings behind an explicit logger that survives the drop.

title: Every authenticated request pays a transaction + set_config GUC round-trip
evidence: rls_context.rs:44 `state.pool.begin()` and :46-52 `SELECT set_config('app.user_id', $1, true), set_config('app.user_role', $2, true)` executed before the handler for every request with an extractable user (guard at :40).
detail: Accurately described. Every authenticated request opens a BEGIN, runs the two-arg set_config query, and commits — a fixed per-request latency floor even for read-only endpoints. This is the deliberate ADR 0011 defense-in-depth posture, not a defect. Correctly classified info.
rec: None required; if a specific high-QPS read endpoint becomes latency-bound, consider a BYPASSRLS read path or folding set_config into the first handler query rather than a separate round-trip.
